title: PHP Cookies
content:
### PHP Cookies

Cookies are small text files stored on the client's browser that persist across page requests.

### Setting a Cookie

```php
<?php
// setcookie(name, value, expire, path, domain, secure, httponly)
setcookie("username", "John", time() + 3600, "/"); // Expires in 1 hour
setcookie("theme", "dark", time() + (86400 * 30), "/"); // 30 days
?>
```

### Accessing Cookies

```php
<?php
if (isset($_COOKIE["username"])) {
    echo "Welcome back, " . $_COOKIE["username"];
} else {
    echo "Welcome, guest";
}
?>
```

### Deleting a Cookie

```php
<?php
// Set expiration in the past
setcookie("username", "", time() - 3600, "/");

// Or simply
setcookie("theme", "", time() - 1);
?>
```

### Cookie Parameters

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `name` | Cookie name |
| `value` | Cookie value |
| `expire` | Unix timestamp for expiration |
| `path` | Server path (default: current) |
| `domain` | Domain (default: current) |
| `secure` | Only send over HTTPS |
| `httponly` | Not accessible via JavaScript |

> **Key Point:** `setcookie()` must be called before any HTML output. Cookies are stored on the client and should never store sensitive data like passwords.
